exercises.prompt: What did generated human faces look like in the earliest days of GANs before StyleGAN?
exercises.explain: Before StyleGAN, early GANs could only manage tiny, blurry, and obviously distorted face outlines. StyleGAN changed the game by scaling up to crystal-clear details!

cards.body: In 2019, a website called 'This Person Does Not Exist' went viral. Millions of people clicked refresh to see endless, perfectly realistic human faces that were completely made up by an AI called StyleGAN!
cards.title: The Face That Wasn't There

cards.body: StyleGAN is a generative model architecture that separates image generation into different 'styles' (like shape, hair color, and expression) at different resolution levels, allowing for unprecedented control and photorealism.
cards.title: What is StyleGAN?

cards.body: Imagine painting a portrait using pre-made stamps of eyes, noses, and hairstyles, but you can adjust the size, color, and texture of each stamp separately instead of drawing the whole face in one messy brushstroke.
cards.title: The Stencil Artist
Style Control in Action
cards.body: If you want to generate a photo of a person with curly hair, StyleGAN lets you inject the 'curly hair style' at a middle layer of the network without changing the person's identity or skin tone.

cards.title: Not a Simple Collage
cards.body: You might think StyleGAN just copies and pastes parts of real photos together. In reality, it builds brand-new faces from scratch by understanding how styles like lighting, pose, and features combine!

left.text: Generates images all at once with limited control over specific features.

right.text: Adjusts features like expression, hair, and lighting at separate layers.

cards.title: Direct Mapping vs. Style Control
cards.body: Look at how generative modeling evolved from the early, rigid days to the fluid style-based control of StyleGAN.

options.text: It controls different visual features at separate resolution layers
It copies pixels directly from real training photos
It only works on black and white cartoon drawings

exercises.explain: StyleGAN works by separating styles at different resolution layers, allowing you to modify features like hair or pose without ruining the entire image structure.
exercises.prompt: How does StyleGAN achieve such realistic and customizable images?

exercises.explain: True! StyleGAN creates completely synthetic images of people who do not exist in the real world.
exercises.prompt: True or False: The faces generated by StyleGAN represent fake people who have never existed.

exercises.explain: StyleGAN uses style-based generators to customize features independently at different levels of detail.
exercises.sentence: StyleGAN shifted generative modeling away from direct mapping to [blank]-based control.
exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: StyleGAN shifted generative modeling away from direct mapping to _______-based control.
Which of these is a classic sign of an early StyleGAN-generated portrait?

options.text: A portrait where the person looks real, but their background contains weird, melting shapes and asymmetrical earrings.
A pixelated retro video game sprite of a space alien.

exercises.explain: Weird background artifacts (like distorted ears, floating earrings, or warped backgrounds) are classic giveaways of early StyleGAN-generated faces.

options.text: High-resolution photorealism with independent control over styles like hair and pose
Faster training speeds but much lower resolution output
The ability to run without any training data

exercises.prompt: What major milestone did StyleGAN bring to the world of generative adversarial networks?
exercises.explain: StyleGAN proved that GANs could scale up to generate highly detailed, photorealistic images by separating styles at different layers. Next up: Wasserstein GAN.

concept: StyleGAN achieved photorealism indistinguishable from real human photos, shifting generative modeling to style-based control. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Wasserstein GAN.'
title: The 'This Person Does Not Exist' Era
